label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. I need to determine whether a typical person would say the hedgehog caused the problem in this scenario. First, let me understand the scenario clearly. There's a box of colored pencils. Only bears are allowed to take pencils, and hedgehogs aren't. The hedgehog wasn't present when this rule was announced, so it doesn't know the new norm. Both the bear and the hedgehog took pencils from the box six times each, alternating. Then, they both took a pencil at the same time. After that, a polar bear came to get a pencil for homework, but there were none left. The question is whether the hedgehog caused the problem of the polar bear not having a pencil for homework. Let me think about the sequence of events: 1. The rule is that only bears can take pencils, and the hedgehog doesn't know this (because it was absent when the rule was announced). 2. The bear and the hedgehog alternately took pencils six times each. So, 6 times for the bear and 6 times for the hedgehog, totaling 12 times. 3. Then, they both took a pencil at the same time. 4. Now, the polar bear comes and finds no pencils left to do homework. So the total number of pencils taken would be: 6 (bear) + 6 (hedgehog) + 1 (bear) + 1 (hedgehog) = 14 pencils. The question
